refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall ensure teleoperation communication latency p95 ≤ 150 ms end-to-end under agreed network conditions to enable safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the original FR to explicitly state the latency metric and end-to-end scope clarifies the performance target and safety relevance.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure end-to-end latency of teleoperation commands and feedback under representative network conditions and verify p95 ≤ 150 ms.
sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency in the teleoperation data path.
FR2: Ensure reliable network link performance to support latency requirements.
FR3: Provide timely telemetry and tasking data exchange with minimal processing delay.

candidate_dps: DP1: Optimize communication protocols and data encoding for low latency.
DP2: Use dedicated network link with QoS guarantees.
DP3: Implement edge processing modules to reduce data processing delays.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Optimize communication protocols and data encoding for low latency.

potential_sources: Coupling between communication protocol optimization and network link performance.
Dependency on telemetry and tasking data formats.
coupling_check.notes: DP1 logically addresses the latency requirement with minimal cross-domain coupling; network link QoS is a constraint rather than a design parameter here.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network link can meet baseline QoS requirements.
Risk that protocol optimization alone may not achieve latency target if network conditions degrade.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating DP2 if network link performance is insufficient or variable.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: All software and hardware components responsible for teleoperation communication and control.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interface for sending and receiving teleoperation data over the network.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Input of vehicle telemetry data for remote operation feedback.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Output of control commands to the vehicle.

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment of latency-critical functions; low diffusion as functionality is centralized.
Vehicle processes telemetry and executes commands but does not control latency optimization.
Safety services monitor but do not implement teleoperation latency functions.
Broad system scope but less focused containment of latency-specific functions.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Latency optimization functions are best contained within the Teleoperation module to minimize diffusion and enable focused ownership.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on network link performance outside Teleoperation control.
Risk of latency-critical code duplication if other modules implement related functions.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Ensure clear interface contracts and monitoring with Network Link and Vehicle modules to manage external dependencies.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP logically satisfies the refined FR with clear latency optimization focus and manageable coupling.

diffusion_risks: Low diffusion risk as latency functions are centralized in Teleoperation module.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores support Teleoperation as the best ownership candidate.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Latency can be measured end-to-end across Teleoperation and Network Link interfaces.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of latency responsibility to Teleoperation module with defined external interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Monitor network link QoS as a constraint and consider fallback DP if network conditions degrade.
Clarify interface contracts to avoid function duplication.

decision.justification: While the current decomposition is sound, the high safety criticality and network dependency require explicit sub-FRs and potential DP integration for network QoS to mitigate risks.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency in the teleoperation data path.
FR2: Ensure reliable network link performance to support latency requirements.
FR3: Provide timely telemetry and tasking data exchange with minimal processing delay.

revise.why: To explicitly capture network link reliability and telemetry/tasking timeliness as separate functional requirements for clearer DP and IM mapping.

assumptions: Network link meets baseline QoS requirements.
Latency optimization can be achieved primarily via protocol and encoding improvements.
open_risks: Potential function diffusion if interface contracts are not enforced.
evidence_needed: Latency measurement data under representative network conditions.
